title: Oil prices jump and Asian shares slip as US and Iran carry out airstrikes
summary: Oil prices jumped and Asian shares were mostly lower Monday after the U.S. carried out airstrikes and Iran retaliated.  The price of Brent crude, the international standard, gained 3.9% to $78.96 per barrel, while U.S. benchmark crude oil added 4% to $74.26 per barrel.  Prices for both types of crude oil recently had slipped back to the levels they were at before the war with Iran began, after the two sides set an interim agreement on ending the conflict and ships resumed transporting oil through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Here’s How Nancy Pelosi Beat the Stock Market and Warren Buffett
summary: The S&P 500 has delivered roughly 13% annualized returns over the past decade, one of the strongest bull runs in modern history. Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK-A)(NYSE:BRK-B), long the gold standard for patient capital, has matched that pace with about 13% annually over the same stretch. Impressive numbers for most investors. Yet both look like amateur hour next to the woman still serving out her final term in Congress: Nancy Pelosi.
